For kitchen & bath shops

Your free 30-minute game plan

Thirty minutes with me, and you walk off with a plan to win back the jobs you’re losing online. The kind of plan I’d normally only put together for a paying client.

It’s for owners who do good work and are done watching it go to shops that do worse.

Quick and easy, I promise. First off, what do I call you?

Good to meet you, there. What’s the best email for you?

Thanks there. A few quick questions so I know whether I’m the right guy for your shop.

First: is your work mostly for homeowners, or builder and contractor accounts?

It’s the one that tells me fast if I’m your guy. Mostly wholesale and I’ll say so on the call instead of taking your money.

Where does most of your work come from right now? Pick all that apply.

There, what’s your website? If you don’t have one yet, just type “don’t have one.”

In a sentence or two, what’s the shop known for, and what’s a typical job worth?

There, be straight with me. What’s the one thing about getting found and getting picked online that’s eating at you most?

Don’t tidy it up. Give me the real version, in your own words. It’s the first thing I read before we talk.

Roughly how many kitchens or baths do you run in a month?

I ask so what I tell you is actually worth your money. Under a certain size, hiring me doesn’t pay off, and I’d rather you hear that now.

How soon are you trying to sort this out?

Last easy one. Where’d you first come across me?

You made it, there. Last one.

I run every session myself and I block the time for you. If you book, will you show up, or give me a heads-up if something comes up?

Looks like a fit, there. Last step is your details, then you’ll pick a time on the calendar.

Shane Nguyen · Kitchen Websites

30-Minute Strategy Session

🕧 30 min📞 Phone call

Claim your free, no-obligation 30-minute strategy session. Straight talk, a real plan, and a clear next step. Pick whatever time works.

P.S. Nothing on the calendar that works for you? Reach me here.

Open the calendar in a new tab →

You’re on the books

You’re on the books, there.

Check your email for the time and the call link. Before we talk, I’ll dig into your shop’s setup so we don’t waste a minute playing catch-up.

See you then.
— Shane
